news.headline: Most shots? Best dribbler? World Cup so far in numbers
news.desc: Now every team has played once, BBC Sport take a look at who has been the best and worst players so far according to the stats.

news.headline: Tactical analysis: England look exciting but how can they tighten up?
news.desc: England produced some exciting attacking football while beating Croatia 4-2 but why did they look so shaky defensively?

news.headline: Kane is England's greatest striker, says Lineker
news.desc: Gary Lineker thinks Harry Kane is "the greatest English striker we've ever had" after the England captain equalled his record of 10 World Cup goals.

news.headline: The celebration photo that sums up my relationship with Mbappe - Giroud
news.desc: France's World Cup winner Olivier Giroud explains why iconic Les Bleus striker Kylian Mbappe is misunderstood, and seen as arrogant when he's not.

news.headline: 'My brother hid in a rice sack' - The refugee stars at the World Cup
news.desc: Germany and Real Madrid defender Antonio Rudiger, whose family fled Sierra Leone's civil war, is among those campaigning for a change in global attitudes around refugees.

news.headline: Hooked after an hour - did Vuskovic boost his Spurs chances?
news.desc: Many Tottenham fans will have got a first glimpse of Luka Vuskovic last night - so what can they expect from the highly sought-after defender?

news.headline: Why does Scotland's McGinn do goggles celebration?
news.desc: John McGinn opens up about the relative who inspired his goal celebration.

news.headline: Ivory Coast's Wahi denied entry to Canada
news.desc: The Ivory Coast football federation have announced their forward Elye Wahi has been denied entry to Canada for their next World Cup game.

news.headline: Hydration boos and surprise results - World Cup talking points
news.desc: BBC Sport takes a look at the talking points from the first week of World Cup.

news.headline: What's going on with stoppage time at the World Cup?
news.desc: Pierluigi Collina decided to wage war on time-wasting at this World Cup, changing tack from the last tournament. But has it been a success so far?

news.headline: Why are England playing a darts song at the World Cup?
news.desc: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the range of goal songs being used at the 2026 World Cup

news.headline: From one to 48 - every World Cup team ranked after first game
news.desc: All 48 teams at the World Cup have now played once. BBC Sport's experts have ranked them all, from best to worst. Who is top? Not defending champions Argentina.
